reasoning: Taylor Swift's fictional/hypothetical latest 2025 album 'The Life of a Showgirl' was released on October 3, 2025. It debuted at #1 on the Billboard 200 with 4M units. By its 5th week, its equivalent album units had rapidly decayed to 120,000. In order to achieve 14 straight weeks at #1, it would need to persist in the #1 position through the entire holiday season (Nov/Dec) and well into January 2026. However, its 5th-week drops (120k) are vastly worse than her own 2024 album 'The Tortured Poets Department', which had 378,000 units in its 5th week. TTPD eventually only pulled off 12 consecutive weeks before Eminem's drop unseated it. Given Showgirl's weaker legs, it will inevitably dip far below the threshold needed to withstand the massive wave of Christmas albums (Michael Bublé, Mariah Carey, Brenda Lee, etc.) that typically dominate the Billboard 200 during December, let alone major new Q4 standard releases. There is virtually no chance the album stays #1 straight through 14 weeks. I am therefore highly confident this resolves NO.
